Q1.notes: Produced 4 valid groups (A/F, N/F, N/O, R/F) with reasonable aggregate values. Total count_order sums to 5,916,591 rows, consistent with ~6M lineitem rows in SF1 dataset.

Q3.notes: Correctly returned 10 rows (LIMIT 10) with positive revenue values. Results properly ordered by revenue descending (406,181.01 to 367,371.15). All dates in early 1995 as expected.

Q6.notes: Produced a single positive revenue value as expected. Fast execution time indicates efficient filtering.

summary: All evaluation steps passed successfully. The generated code compiled without errors or warnings, loaded all required TPC-H tables (6M+ lineitem rows, 1.5M orders, 150K customers), and executed all three queries correctly. Q1 produced 4 valid groupings with accurate aggregates, Q3 returned the top 10 results ordered by revenue, and Q6 calculated a single revenue value. Execution times were reasonable (33-198ms per query). The implementation demonstrates correct query logic, proper data loading, and efficient execution.
